message:
fix(concurrency_limit): register semaphore waiter without thread-local Cx

When poll_ready finds all permits taken and no thread-local Cx exists,
the middleware previously returned Poll::Pending without registering a
waker with the semaphore, leaving the service permanently asleep until
an external caller happened to re-poll it.

Now uses OwnedAcquireFuture::new_uncancelable() as a fallback when Cx
is unavailable, which properly enqueues a semaphore waiter and wakes
the task when a permit becomes available.

New test helpers:
- CountingWaker: tracks wake() invocations for assertions
- NeverCompleteService: a service whose call() never resolves

New test pending_without_current_cx_registers_waiter_and_wakes_on_release
verifies that dropping a held permit wakes the blocked waiter even
without an ambient Cx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
